name: canonify
description: How to operate Canonify declaratively. Read SKILL.md first, then use the resources to deep-dive on a specific declaration verb, the handler DSL, or the error-code contract.

resources.title: Declaring tables
resources.description: Use schema.propose_migration to create tables. Auto-CRUD synthesises the default ObjectType + create/read/update/delete/list actions from the table shape.

resources.title: Declaring ObjectTypes
resources.description: Use catalog.refine_object_type to narrow string columns to enums, surface FK links, set on_delete cascade, and override hidden columns.

resources.title: Declaring StateMachines
resources.description: Use state_machines.register to bind a Phase 18.5 FSM to an ObjectType enum property. Transitions name the Action that drives them; on_enter/on_exit hooks enqueue follow-up actions.

resources.title: Declaring custom ActionTypes
resources.description: Use actions.declare to register a custom multi-step ActionType. The handler is a JSON DSL — typed CRUD steps over ObjectTypes glued by the $ref grammar — executed inside the runtime's tx + audit + outbox + FSM frame.

resources.title: Handler DSL cookbook
resources.description: Copy-paste-ready handler examples — simple update, multi-table atomic (lead.convert), returns-block patterns. Every example decodes cleanly against the Phase 17.5 HandlerDsl effect/Schema.

resources.title: Error codes
resources.description: Every ActionDomainError kind, when it fires, the agent's recovery hint, and how the envelope's error field renders it.
